title: Executive Assistant
description:
EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT (Female only, Full-Time, Remote – Philippines)



Join a mission-driven company empowering women across Asia to live stronger, healthier, and happier lives.

We’re growing fast — and we’re looking for a highly organised, proactive, and loyal Executive Assistant to support our leadership team and keep daily operations running like clockwork. 



How it looks like to be on this team: 



This is a long-term, full-time position (not freelance) with a company that values your growth, integrity, and professional excellence. 



???? YOUR ROLE



You’ll work directly with our senior leadership team, helping coordinate schedules, manage communications, and streamline key projects.



Your main responsibilities:



* Manage daily schedules, calendars, and appointments

* Handle professional
s — screen, prioritise, and draft replies

* Track projects, tasks, and documentation accurately

* Conduct research and prepare executive reports or presentations

* Coordinate between departments (Ops, Sales, Coaches, HR)

* Assist with HR, onboarding, and internal communications

* Manage travel logistics and event planning (as needed)

* Uphold confidentiality and professionalism at all times



 ???? WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING WITH US



? 95% Remote Work — eliminate long commutes, enjoy more balance

? Competitive KPI-based pay— reward tied to real performance

? Stable, long-term role — this is a career path, not a gig

? Clear SOPs & structured onboarding to set you up for success

? Supportive leadership that values feedback and transparency

? Collaborative, growth-driven culture — your voice matters here

? Training & development — we invest in your continuous learning

? Respectful, integrity-first workplace



???? YOU’RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:



* Excellent English communication skills— written & spoken

* Typing speed of at least 45 WPM

* Strong organisational and problem-solving skills

* Experience with Google Workspace, MS Office, and online tools

* Proactive attitude and impeccable attention to detail

* Reliable internet connection with backup plan

* Quiet, professional home workspace with webcam and lighting

* Prior experience as Executive Assistant / Admin / VA is a plus

* A personality grounded in honesty, initiative, and consistency



???? NOT A GOOD FIT IF YOU:



* Have other part
 work

* Unfamiliar with basic remote work tech set up (google suite, zoom etc)

* Struggle with deadlines or frequent absences

* Need constant reminders or supervision

* Complain when things get challenging instead of problem-solving

* Lack a stable workspace or internet connection



We need someone who shows up, communicates clearly, and takes ownership.



???? WORK HOURS



Tuesday to Saturday, 1:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Singapore time)

This ensures smooth coordination with our Singapore-based team.



???? OUR COMPANY CULTURE



We live by five core values that define everything we do:



* Honesty — tell the truth, always.

* Integrity — deliver with excellence and accuracy.

* Commitment — follow through on your word.

* Growth Mindset — keep learning, keep improving.

* Team Spirit — work with respect, collaboration, and positivity.



???? HOW TO APPLY



Send an
 with the subject line: “Personal Executive Assistant” and include clear answers to ALL of the following:



1?? Why should we hire you? Describe your experience — don’t list it.

2?? Where in the Philippines are you based, and how stable is your power/internet?

3?? What equipment do you use (computer specs, internet speed)?

4?? What’s your plan if there’s a power or internet outage?

5?? Record a short audio intro — share your name, favourite food, and favourite place to visit!

6?? Attach a screenshot of your DISC profile from OnlineJobs.ph.

7?? Take a typing test from 
  and include your screenshot result.



???? READY TO GROW WITH US?



If you’re seeking long-term stability, a positive and structured team culture, and the chance to grow in a meaningful role — we’d love to hear from you.



Apply now and become part of a company that values excellence, growth, and integrity. 



Warm regards

Terry Lim

HR Manager

title: Influencer Research & Outreach VA (NO EXPERIENCE NEEDED) - 8
description:
TURN YOUR SOCIAL MEDIA SCROLLING INTO A REMOTE JOB (Influencer Research + DM VA)

If you spend way too much time on Instagram/TikTok checking gym creators, this might be the easiest job you ever land.



We’re a Gen-Z–run app studio building a fast-growing fitness app, and we’re hiring VAs to help us research, reach out to, and build relationships with influencers.



- No experience required.

- No resume required.

- Just strong English + good judgment + social media instincts.

- Must work NYC timezones! (in between 9am to 5pm)



If you understand gym culture, talk like a human, and type like a native English speaker, you’re already ahead of 99% of applicants.





WHAT YOU’LL DO

This role is part lead generation, part DM outreach, part relationship management.



Your daily tasks:

- Find new fitness creators (gym/lifting niche only)

- Add them to our Google Sheet with the correct info (username, followers, country,
, etc.)

- Evaluate their profile quality (are they legit, on-brand, and worth reaching out to?)

- DM influencers using our scripts

- Handle the conversation in a friendly, natural, professional way

- Answer questions, keep the convo alive, and push creators toward collaboration

- Follow up consistently

- Track responses and organize leads properly



You’ll basically be the person discovering new influencers and bringing them into the brand.

We’ll teach you everything, you just need strong English and social media instincts.





YOU’RE A FIT IF YOU

- Have very strong written English (this is the #1 requirement)

- Can communicate clearly and confidently

- Know the gym/fitness niche (huge bonus)

- Are organized and detail-oriented

- Can follow instructions EXACTLY

- Are comfortable reaching out to people on social media

- Have good judgment (can spot fake/farm accounts instantly)

- Are under 28 (our team is young)

- Want part-time remote work you can do from anywhere



We don’t care about degrees. We care about output, consistency, and attitude.





WHAT WE OFFER

- Remote work

- Fast replies, fast onboarding

- Fun Gen-Z team

- Simple tasks anyone smart can do

- Performance-based promotions

- The chance to handle creator deals + negotiations once you prove yourself





TEST 1 (WHEN APPLIYING) — Influencer Sheet Review + Research



Use this Google Sheet template (make a copy of the sheet to modify it): 
/drive/folders/1hDVQyxHEi-ZmaKfmGk1KWSzqrxKSM-Q7?usp=sharing



You must complete:

Rows 3–13 — PASS or FAIL



Look at the creators already listed and ONLY fill the PASS / FAIL column (1st column of the table).

Base your decision on:

- Fitness/gym relevance (are they a gym influencer?)

- Their country (our target audience is North American countries and 1st world countries)

- High number of views (100 000 median views minimum)

*Little tip, less than 50% are good :)*



Rows 14–23 — Add 10 New Fitness Creators (based on the same criterias that were used for rows 3-13)

Add:

- Username

- Instagram profile link

- Number of followers

- Median views per reel (approximately)

- Full name

-
 (if public)

- Country



These must be real gym/lifting creators. No deep digging. Just fast and accurate.





TEST 2 (AFTER YOU GOT SELECTED FOR THE 2ND ROUND) — 1-3 Minutes Introduction Video



Record a simple phone video (no editing needed) and answer:

- Who you are

- Any experience with customer support jobs, social media or research

- How comfortable you are chatting with influencers through DMs

- Why you’re a good fit for this role



Just speak clearly and naturally.





HOW TO APPLY

Reply to this job post with:

A Google Drive link containing:

- Your completed Google Sheet (for TEST 1 tasks)



Answers to these short questions (in text form in the message you send when applying):

- How many hours/day or week can you work?

- Are you comfortable with NYC TIME hours full-time?

- Are you comfortable handling DM conversations?



Applications missing either test 1 OR answers to the questions will be ignored automatically.

Write “Philippines #1” at the end of your last question's answer so we know you actually read this :)

title: Senior Marketing & Brand Coordinator
description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a creative, strategic, and "design-first" Marketing Coordinator to lead the digital presence of two social care organisations: a boutique residential care home group and a specialist complex homecare provider based in the UK.



You will be the "Brand Guardian," responsible for ensuring our services look premium, feel trustworthy, and generate consistent private-pay enquiries. This is a hands-on role where you will design brochures, manage websites, rank us on Google, and tell the stories of our residents and staff.



Key Responsibilities

1. Brand & Creative Design

Visual Identity: Manage and evolve the visual branding for both companies across all touchpoints (brochures, signage, digital assets).



Graphic Design: Use Canva (or Adobe Suite) to create high-end marketing collateral that appeals to self-funding families and clinical case managers.



Content Production: Transform raw photos/videos from our UK teams into engaging social media content and high-quality video clips.



2. Digital Growth & SEO

Search Dominance: Manage and optimize Google Business Profiles for multiple locations to ensure we rank #1 in local searches.



SEO & Website: Maintain and update our WordPress websites. Write SEO-optimized blogs that position us as thought leaders in complex care and dementia.



Paid Ads: Execute and monitor Meta and Google Ad campaigns when required to boost occupancy.



3. Reputation & Trust

Review Management: Monitor and respond professionally to reviews on Google and 
 to build "Social Proof."



Persuasive Copywriting: Write "Life Story" features, newsletters, and website copy that balances clinical excellence with emotional warmth.



4. Lead Generation

Inbound Strategy: Implement organic strategies to turn website visitors into phone enquiries.



Market Positioning: Ensure our "Boutique" status is reflected in every digital post, differentiating us from large corporate competitors.



Requirements & Skills

Experience: 3+ years in a multi-channel marketing role (Healthcare or Real Estate experience is a huge plus).



Exceptional taste in design and a portfolio that proves you can create premium, clean, and modern layouts.



Tech Stack: Proficiency in Canva, Meta Business Suite, Google Ads, WordPress, and Video Editing (CapCut/Premiere).



Language: Good written English with a talent for persuasive, empathetic storytelling.



SEO Savvy: A solid understanding of local SEO and how to rank a service-based business.



AI Proficiency: Ability to use AI tools (Gemini, Nanobanana ) to accelerate content creation without losing the "human touch."

Ability to work during UK business hours (GMT 09:00 - 17:00) Mon - Friday



How to Apply (3-Step Process)

To be considered for this role, you must complete the following three steps:



Your CV: Attach your latest CV highlighting relevant service-based marketing experience.



Introduction Video: Record a short 2-minute video introducing yourself. We want to see your personality, your communication style, and why you think you’re a great fit.



The Proposal: Find us online (JT Carehomes). Review our current website and social media presence. Provide a short PDF or slide deck (max 3-5 pages) showing:



What you would change immediately to make us look more "Premium/Boutique."



A mood board or mockup of what our Instagram/Facebook grid should look like.



One SEO or ranking "quick win" you would implement for our Google Business Profile.



Please send the above to

title: Real Estate Video/Photograpy Editor
description:
About Us

Bolder Digital is an Australian-based recruitment agency committed to connecting top Filipino talent with leading businesses globally. We are looking for an individual to join our team and work remotely from the Philippines as we are based in Australia (Remote, based in the Philippines, working on Australian Eastern Time Zone 8/9 AM – 5 PM).



Job Type

Full-Time – Long term/Gig



Start Date:

ASAP



Compensation:

Negotiable — based on proven expertise



Overview

We are hiring an elite Video Editor with advanced, professional-level experience in real estate, construction, architecture, and promotional brand content.



This role requires someone who has spent years working in high-pressure commercial environments — someone who understands tight turnarounds, precision editing, narrative flow, and the visual standards required in premium marketing.



You’ll be working across:

High-end real estate property videos

Builder & construction showcases

Architectural features & design storytelling

Promotional brand edits for businesses

If you don’t have industry-proven commercial experience, don’t apply.

If you do — we want you.



Key Responsibilities

Real Estate, Construction & Promo Editing

Edit cinematic, high-impact property videos showcasing interiors, exteriors, lifestyle elements, architecture, and land features

Build fast-turnaround construction progress videos, promo reels, project showcases, and brand storytelling pieces

Craft engaging short-form edits for Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts using clean pacing, crisp sound design, and strong narrative structure

Masterfully repurpose long-form shoots into multiple content deliverables

Apply brand identity, graphics, typography, colour grading, and transitions with consistency and precision

Creative Execution & Collaboration

Work from raw media and briefs to produce polished, broadcast-level edits

Take constructive criticism without ego — improve, iterate, and refine

Work efficiently under pressure, meeting tight deadlines and high-volume demands

Communicate clearly with our internal team and clients across review cycles



Optional but Preferred

Photo editing experience in Photoshop & Lightroom

Ability to design static promotional assets (carousels, quotes, graphic posts)

Strong understanding of brand identity and visual strategy



Portfolio Requirements

Submit 10–15 examples of:

Real estate, construction, or architectural video edits

Promotional commercial edits

Short-form vertical content (Reels, Shorts, TikTok)

(Optional) Static design work

For each item, include your typical editing timeframe (e.g. “average time to edit a 2-minute real estate showcase” or “average time for a 30-second promo reel”).

Applicants who cannot provide a portfolio at this level will not be considered.



Requirements

Expert-level skill in Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects. (Final Cut or DaVinci Resolve acceptable for masters only.)

Minimum 3–5+ years professional experience in real estate/construction/promotional editing

Strong understanding of modern pacing, visual hooks, retention strategy, and commercial storytelling

Ability to work fast without compromising quality

Willingness to learn, grow, adapt — no ego

Availability during Australian Eastern Time working hours

Reliable internet + hardware capable of smooth high-res workflows



Additional Preferences

Strong colour grading ability

Understanding of real estate marketing strategy

Familiarity with aerial/drone workflow

Ability to contribute ideas to improve visual strategy



Benefits

Competitive pay based on expertise

Flexible working structure

Long-term, consistent work

High-level creative environment with room to grow



How to Apply

Submit:

Resume

Short cover letter

A portfolio with required samples

Your average editing times per content type

Use the application code VIDDY so we know you’ve read the full role description.



IMPORTANT: This is an expert-only position. You will be skills-tested.

If you’re not proven, not fast, or not world-class — this isn’t for you.

 and Instagram comments and messages

 lead conversion

title: Image Processing Assistant – Resize and Format Travel Photos for Website
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented Image Formatting Assistant to prepare travel photos for use on a website.



Another researcher will first collect high-quality images and organize them into folders. Your job will be to take those images and process them into a consistent format that is ready to be used on the website.



This role focuses on cropping, resizing, and exporting images so they all follow the same visual standard.



The work is structured and repetitive, so attention to detail and the ability to follow instructions carefully is important.

You should be comfortable using image editing software such as Photoshop, Affinity Photo, GIMP, or similar tools.



Responsibilities



Your main responsibilities will include:



• Open raw images provided by our research assistant

• Crop images to highlight the main subject or landscape

• Resize images to a fixed website canvas size

• Export images in a consistent high-quality format

• Save the processed images into the correct folders



The goal is to create clean, consistent, website-ready images.



Image Processing Requirements



All images must follow these formatting rules.



Canvas Size

All final images must be resized to:

2560 × 1440 pixels (16:9 landscape format)

Images should be cropped to fit this canvas without stretching or distortion.



Image Quality



Images should be exported as:

• JPEG format

• High quality 

• sRGB color profile

Images must remain sharp and visually clean, while still being optimized for website performance.



Cropping Guidelines



When cropping images, focus on:



• the main landscape or scenery

• the destination or activity

• balanced composition



Avoid awkward crops that remove important parts of the scene.



File Naming Format



Images must maintain this format:



location_name_01.jpg

location_name_02.jpg

location_name_03.jpg



Examples:



tulum_ruins_01.jpg

akumal_bay_01.jpg

cenote_dos_ojos_01.jpg



Use underscores instead of spaces.



Folder Structure



Processed images will be saved in organized folders such as:



• Beaches & Snorkelling

• Cenotes

• Mayan Ruins

• Scuba Diving

• Parks & Nature



Each destination will have its own folder.



Quality Expectations



Processed images must be:



• properly cropped

• consistently sized

• clearly named

• saved in the correct folder



Attention to detail is extremely important for this role.



Project Scope



This project will involve preparing images for 100+ travel destinations.

Each destination will typically require 5–10 final formatted images.

If the work quality is good, additional similar work may be available.



raw images will appear in this folder - 





processed images will go here -

title: VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION
description:
VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION



We're a growing YouTube channel covering AI, automation, robotics, and emerging technology. We're hiring 2 dedicated video editors for full-time, long-term positions.



This is not a side gig or one-off freelance project. We're building something real, and we're looking for editors who want to grow with the channel long-term. If you're early in your career, hungry to build a serious portfolio, and ready to commit to consistent, high-volume work, this is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor of a channel that's scaling fast. The editors who prove themselves here will have a clear path to expanded roles, higher pay, and real ownership over production.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



Editing 3 long-form YouTube videos per week (9 to 22 minutes each) plus 1 vertical short (30 to 35 seconds). That's 4 deliverables per week. Each long-form video follows a detailed Editor's Table, a scene-by-scene visual blueprint that tells you exactly what goes on screen at every timecode. Every shot type, every B-roll asset, every visual composition, every documentation link is mapped out for you. You'll never have to guess what the vision is. Your job is to execute it at a high level with creative energy, polished visuals, and dynamic pacing.



Each video requires a visual change every 5 seconds. Zooms, cuts, motion graphics, text animations, transitions, split-screen compositions, kinetic text moments, and data overlays. This is fast-paced, high-retention editing designed to keep viewers locked in.



The vertical short is pulled from the best clip across your 3 long-form videos. After probation, this increases to 1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week).





BREAKING NEWS PRIORITY



We also cover breaking AI news. When a major story drops, you may need to reprioritize your current workload and turn around a breaking news video as fast as possible. This means pausing whatever you're working on and editing the urgent video first. Speed and flexibility matter here. If you can't handle shifting priorities on short notice, this role isn't for you.





WHAT'S PROVIDED VS. WHAT YOU BUILD



Provided by us:



All A-roll footage (AI avatar talking head). You do NOT film or source A-roll.



An Editor's Handbook, your permanent reference guide covering every production standard, shot type definition, B-roll category, branded glitch placement, hook rules, avatar cleanup pipeline, and quality benchmarks. This is your go-to document for how things work on this channel. You'll receive it on day one.



A detailed Editor's Table for every video. A scene-by-scene visual blueprint with exact timecodes, shot types, visual assignments, B-roll tags, Director's Notes, and full spoken dialogue for every scene. You'll never have to guess what the vision is.



Documentation links and screenshot instructions. You capture the screenshots.



Stock footage keywords. You pull the clips from Envato Elements (access included).



After Effects composition directives. You build the compositions from detailed descriptions specifying elements, layout, colors, and animation.



YouTube source video search terms. You source real footage clips using WayinVideo (wayin.ai), a tool that lets you search within YouTube videos to find specific moments.



Quote card text and sources. You design and place them.



Full-screen kinetic text lines. You create bold, impactful typography moments.



Digital Twin Intro clips. Pre-recorded footage for the channel's signature intro sequence that appears at the start of every video. You'll receive the raw clips and build the intro following the Editor's Table direction.



What you build:



You're not sourcing content or coming up with ideas. Everything is mapped out for you in the Editor's Table, and the Editor's Handbook is your permanent reference for standards. But you ARE building the visuals, compositions, and motion graphics that bring it to life. Your creative judgment matters. How you cut, how you pace, how you build visual energy between scenes is your craft. The Editor's Table is the blueprint. You bring it to life.





THE 40/60 SPLIT



About 40% of each video features the AI avatar on screen. The remaining 60% is full-screen B-roll: custom After Effects compositions, documentation screenshots, source video footage, stock footage, quote cards, and kinetic text. A visual change must happen every 5 seconds. No static frames.





AVATAR SHOT TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



You'll work with five shot types that rotate throughout every video:



Full Frame. Pure avatar, direct to camera. Maximum 5 to 6 seconds per clip. Used sparingly at scene endings for emotional payoff.



Split Panel. Avatar on one side (~40% of frame), data/documentation/visual on the other (~60%). This is the primary delivery mode. The panel content changes every 5 seconds.



With Overlay. Avatar visible but kinetic text, stat bars, or lower thirds appear on screen. The viewer's eye is drawn to the data. Maximum 2 per scene.



Zoom Punch. Quick 1 to 2 second tight crop cut on the avatar's face for punchlines and pivots. Target 6 to 8 per video minimum.



PIP Float. Small circular avatar cutout (~30 to 35% of frame) floating over full-screen source footage. Features an orbiting glow trail around the circle edge. Used when real YouTube footage plays underneath the avatar's voice. Target 5 to 7 per video. Can run longer than other shot types (8 to 15 seconds).





B-ROLL TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



Every visual in the Editor's Table is tagged with one of six categories:



B.R1, Documentation: Full-screen screenshot of a source page, article, report, or data dashboard. You open the provided link, screenshot the relevant section, and drop it in. This is proof, showing the viewer real evidence.



B.R2, Authority Quote: Quote card overlay attributed to a specific person. For recognizable public figures, include a photo alongside the quote. 0 to 3 per video.



B.R3, AE Composition: Custom After Effects motion graphics. Headline stacks, timelines, comparison charts, data visualizations, ecosystem maps. You build these from detailed written directives. Maximum 8 per video.



B.R4, Stock Footage: Atmospheric footage from Envato Elements. Used for mood and energy, not proof. Short clips only, 3 to 5 seconds maximum.



B.R5, Kinetic Text: Full-screen animated text moments for key phrases and thesis statements, or lower third name/title/stat bars. Maximum 12 per video.



B.R6a, Source Video: Real YouTube footage sourced via WayinVideo. The Editor's Table provides search terms. You search YouTube for the title, paste the URL into WayinVideo, use "Find Moments" with the provided keywords, select the best clip, and trim. Visual only, always mute source audio. Minimum 8 per video. A visible channel credit 
 must appear on screen for every source video clip.





BRANDED GLITCH (SOUND IDENTITY)



Every video features 4 branded glitches, a signature chromatic RGB split with a digital glitch sound effect, lasting 0.3 seconds. These are the ONLY sound effects in the entire video. No additional SFX on stat reveals, transitions, scene changes, or anywhere else. The branded glitch is the sound identity of the channel. Placement is specified in the Editor's Table.





LIVE-ACTION VIDEOS (OCCASIONAL)



The majority of your work will be AI avatar videos. However, there will be occasions where you'll edit videos featuring the real Francis on camera. For example, hands-on product reviews, studio walkthroughs, or special topic deep dives. These videos still follow the same B-roll editing standards (documentation screenshots, source footage, AE compositions, kinetic text) but with real human footage as the A-roll instead of the avatar. The AI cleanup pipeline does not apply to these videos. Frequency will vary, but expect this as part of the role.





AI AVATAR CLEANUP (CRITICAL)



The AI avatar footage contains synthesis artifacts (temporal flickering, face-body boundary distortion, micro-jitter, and compression ghosting) that are visible on large screens and monitors. Before any editing begins, all avatar A-roll must go through this cleanup pipeline:



1. After Effects, Neat Video: Apply to all avatar A-roll. Reduces temporal flickering and compression artifacts. This is the primary tool for cleaning AI avatar footage.



2. After Effects, RSMB (ReelSmart Motion Blur): Add motion blur between frames. Smooths the micro-jitter that causes the "dizzy" feeling on AI faces.



3. After Effects, Mocha Face Tracking: Track the face region, apply subtle skin noise reduction to face only. Do not over-smooth. Keep it natural.



4. After Effects, Face-Body Seam Cleanup: Gaussian blur 0.5 to 1px on the boundary where AI face meets body/neck. Hides the synthesis seam.



5. Premiere Pro, Color Grade: Consistent Lumetri color grade across all avatar shots. Match color temperature with B-roll.



6. Premiere Pro, Film Grain Overlay: Organic film grain at 5 to 10% opacity over entire video. Grain masks digital artifacts and makes footage feel natural.



You must also be able to identify and mask unnatural AI movements, such as awkward hand gestures or slow, robotic eye blinks, through strategic cuts, zooms, B-roll placement, and motion graphics. The goal is to never let the viewer notice something is off.



If you've worked with AI-generated footage before (HeyGen, Synthesia, or similar), that's a big plus. If not, you must be willing to learn these techniques quickly.





REQUIRED SKILLS



These are non-negotiable. If you can't demonstrate these in your portfolio or test project, this role isn't the right fit.



Adobe Premiere Pro. Confident, daily-driver level.



Adobe After Effects. Must be comfortable building custom compositions from written directives (animated diagrams, comparison layouts, data visualizations, framework graphics, headline stacks, timelines).



Split-screen composition. Building and managing dynamic split-panel layouts where content changes every 5 seconds.



Motion graphics and kinetic text. Bold full-screen typography, animated stat callouts, lower thirds, text reveals.



Color grading. Lumetri color correction, skin tone matching, consistent grade across mixed footage sources.



Noise reduction and artifact cleanup. Neat Video or equivalent temporal noise reduction experience.



Face tracking. Mocha or similar tool for selective face region processing.



AI footage awareness. Ability to spot and fix synthesis artifacts (ghosting, jitter, face-body seam, unnatural movement) through cleanup tools and strategic editing choices.





OTHER REQUIREMENTS



A computer capable of handling 4K video editing.

Reliable internet and consistent availability.

Strong attention to detail.

Ability to meet weekly deadlines (3 long-form videos + 1 short per week).

Comfortable using Asana for task management and team communication. All assignments, feedback, and deadlines are managed through Asana.





COMPENSATION AND GROWTH



$1,000 USD per month paid bi-weekly ($500 every two weeks). This is probationary pay for the first 90 days.



The $1,000 monthly rate is based on delivering all 16 videos per month (12 long-form + 4 shorts). Pay is tied to output. If deliverables are consistently missed or incomplete, compensation will be adjusted accordingly.



We cover the Upwork service fee. You receive the full $1,000.



Guaranteed pay increase after 90 days as long as quality remains high and you pass probation.



Full-time, long-term position with room to grow.



Envato Elements access provided for stock footage and assets.



Entry-level candidates and recent graduates welcome. We value reliability and hunger over years of experience.





POST-PROBATION GROWTH (AFTER 90 DAYS)



This is where things get exciting. After probation, top performers take on expanded responsibilities and higher pay. This includes:



YouTube thumbnail creation (templates and direction provided).

Full YouTube publishing: uploading, metadata, thumbnails, scheduling.

Script development and content creation support.

End-to-end video production ownership.

1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week, up from 1).



Expanded responsibilities come with increased pay. The more you grow, the more you earn. We want editors who see this as a career path, not just a paycheck.





WORKING WITH US. WHAT TO EXPECT



This is a professional working relationship and we want to be upfront about how things work so there are no surprises.



Revisions and Quality Control:

Every video goes through a review process before publishing. You should expect feedback and revision requests on most edits, especially in the first few months as we align on style and standards. Revisions are a normal part of the workflow, not a sign that something went wrong. If something doesn't meet our quality standards, you'll be asked to fix it before it goes live. The goal is to get better together over time.



Confidentiality:

All scripts, workflows, production systems, visual templates, and content strategies you're exposed to through this role are confidential. You may not share, reproduce, or discuss any internal materials, processes, or unreleased content outside of this working relationship. This applies during and after your time with us.



Work Ownership:

All content you create in this role (edits, compositions, motion graphics, templates, shorts, thumbnails) belongs to the channel. You may not reuse, repurpose, or include any work created for us in your personal portfolio or for other clients without written permission.



Availability and Communication:

You're expected to be reachable during agreed working hours via Asana. All assignments, deadlines, feedback, and revisions are managed through Asana, not
, not DMs. When breaking news hits, same-day communication and turnaround may be required. If you're unavailable for an extended period, advance notice is expected.



Publishing Authorization (Post-Probation):

Once you're granted access to the YouTube channel for publishing, you may only upload, schedule, or modify content that has been explicitly approved. No unauthorized uploads, title changes, description edits, or scheduling adjustments without sign-off.





PERFORMANCE STANDARDS AND CONTRACTOR TERMS



This is an independent contractor arrangement through Upwork. This is not an employment relationship and does not include benefits, severance, or employment protections.



During the 90-day probation period, either party may end the arrangement at any time, for any reason, with no notice required.



After probation, we follow a structured feedback process. If quality standards are not being met, deadlines are consistently missed, or communication breaks down, the process is:



1. Verbal feedback identifying the specific issue and what needs to change.

2. Written notice via Asana with a clear improvement timeline.

3. If the issue persists after written notice, the arrangement will be ended.



We are direct about expectations and fair about giving you the chance to improve. That said, certain actions may result in immediate termination without the above process, including but not limited to: breach of confidentiality, unauthorized publishing or modification of channel content, extended unexcused absence without notice, or any conduct that puts the channel's reputation or operations at risk.



We reserve the right to end the arrangement at our discretion. Reasonable notice will be provided whenever possible.



This is designed to be a long-term home. We're not looking to cycle through editors. We want people who grow with us, take ownership, and build something they're proud of. If you bring the work ethic and talent, we'll bring the opportunity, structure, and pay increases to match.





TO APPLY



Link to your portfolio or 2 to 3 sample edits.

Brief description of your editing setup (computer specs, software).

Your availability and timezone.

Short note on why you'd be a good fit.



We're looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and excited to grow with a channel focused on cutting-edge AI content. This is a full-time commitment, ideal for someone early in their career who wants consistent work, a growing portfolio, and a long-term home.

title: YouTube Video Editor / 2.5D Animator
description:
Magnetic Chemistry is a relationship psychology education brand creating faceless YouTube content for women ages 25-45. We use AI-cloned voiceovers (ElevenLabs) and custom 2.5D illustrated characters to produce research-backed relationship insight videos.



We are hiring a video editor to animate our pre-made illustration library and produce publish-ready YouTube videos.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

- Animate pre-made 2.5D character illustrations (layered PNG/PSD assets) with subtle motion — parallax, expressions, transitions, and scene composition

- Sync animated visuals to AI-generated voiceover audio files

- Composite full YouTube videos: animated characters + backgrounds + text overlays + transitions + background music

- Create thumbnails for each video using the brand's asset library

- Deliver raw project files alongside rendered finals



WHAT WE PROVIDE:

- Complete visual asset library: 113 custom-illustrated modular characters, expressions, poses, hairstyles, and backgrounds — all layered and ready for animation

- Finished voiceover audio files for each video

- Detailed editor brief per video with visual direction notes and scene breakdown

- Brand color palette and style guidelines



REQUIRED SKILLS:

- Adobe After Effects (primary tool for character animation and compositing)

- Experience with puppet rigging, parallax animation, or 2.5D motion from layered static illustrations

- Audio sync — matching visual cues and transitions to voiceover timing

- Thumbnail design (Photoshop or Canva)

- Reliable internet and ability to upload/download large files via Google Drive

- English proficiency for understanding briefs and revision communication



PREFERRED (NOT REQUIRED):

- Experience editing faceless YouTube channels or explainer-style animated content

- Motion graphics or kinetic typography skills

- Familiarity with YouTube pacing and visual storytelling



THE OPPORTUNITY:

This starts as a per-video contract. We'll work closely on the first 3 videos to establish workflow and quality standards. After validation, top performers will be offered a monthly retainer with consistent ongoing work as our channel scales. Target volume is 2-3 videos per week. Video length is 8-12 minutes.



TO APPLY, PLEASE INCLUDE:

1. Portfolio or reel showing 2.5D animation or character animation from static assets (YouTube samples ideal)

2. Your per-video rate for an 8-12 minute animated video (our range is $50-$75 USD)

3. Your typical turnaround time per video

4. Your weekly availability (hours)

title: Freelance Video Editor – Meta Ad Creatives
description:
Who we are

Ou brand is a growing online fragrance brand based in the Netherlands. We sell premium perfumes and are actively scaling our advertising on Meta 
 & Instagram). We're looking for a video editor to join our small team for a stable, long-term weekly collaboration.



What the job is

Every week, we film new content. You receive the raw footage and edit it into finished Meta ad creatives. We handle strategy and filming, you focus entirely on editing.

This is simple, repeatable work with a clear weekly rhythm. If you're reliable and your work converts, this is a stable long-term role.



The two formats you'll edit

1. UGC-style short-form videos (15–30 seconds): Authentic, natural-looking content. Lo-fi style: it should feel real, not over-produced.

2. Compilation ads: Multiple clips edited together into one punchy, scroll-stopping ad.



Exactly what you'll do in each video

- Cut and trim clips to the right length and pace

- Assemble footage: b-rolls, UGC clips, product close-ups

- Add transitions between clips

- Add sound effects and background music

- Insert images into the video (Trustpilot review screenshots, product bundles, promo banners)

- Add captions or subtitles



Each video takes around 45–60 minutes to edit. No complex effects or animation needed, clean, fast-paced cuts that make people stop scrolling.



Weekly schedule

We film every Wednesday

You receive a batch of raw footage shortly after: usually 3–5 videos + UGC clips

Batch size varies from week to week

Deadline: finished videos returned by end of the same week

All communication via written brief: no time zone issues, no calls required



Pay

$4–$6 per finished video.

Typical weekly earnings: $15–$30 depending on how many videos are in the batch.

Paid weekly, reliably. We are consistent and we always pay on time.

Editors with a strong ecommerce or beauty portfolio can discuss a higher rate.



What we need from you

- Experience editing short-form social media video (TikTok, Reels,
 ads)

- Portfolio with at least a few ecommerce, beauty, or lifestyle examples

- Reliable with deadlines every week, on time

- Good with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or similar tools

- Able to follow a written brief in English without needing calls

- Comfortable inserting image overlays (review screenshots, product cards) into video



How to apply

Send your application with:

- 2–3 links to videos you've edited, social ads or short-form content preferred

- A short intro: who are you and how long have you been editing?

- Your available hours per week

- 5 stores you have worked for



ATTENTION CHECK Important, please read

- To show you've read this job post carefully, please start your message

with the word "GEUR" (this is the Dutch word for fragrance).

- Then answer this question in 2–3 sentences:

- What is one editing technique you always use to make a short video more engaging?

Messages that don't follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



The top 2–3 applicants will be invited to a paid trial week. We send you 3 real briefings and you edit 3 real videos. At the end of the week we review together and decide if we're a great fit.



We're building something long-term. Great editors who grow with us get consistent work and better rates over time.

title: Facebook Ads Coach / Mentor — Teach Me to Run Profitable Meta Campaigns (Hourly, Part-Time)
description:
I’m a Melbourne-based entrepreneur running an AI-powered business that serves medical spas and aesthetic clinics in Australia. I’m currently running
 lead generation campaigns, but I need to deeply understand the mechanics behind what I’m doing — not just follow templates.



I’m looking for an experienced
 Ads specialist to coach me 1-on-1 via screen share (Google Meet or Zoom). This is NOT a campaign management role. You will NOT be running my ads. Your job is to teach me how to do it myself.



What This Role Looks Like

3 x 1-hour sessions per week via Google Meet or Zoom (days/times flexible to your schedule)

I share my screen showing my live
 Ads Manager account

I click through the settings while you explain the WHY behind each action and strategy

You walk me through campaign structure, ad sets, creative strategy, audience targeting, budget settings, and data analysis

Think of this as a mentor/apprentice dynamic — you teach, I execute, you correct and explain



What I Need You to Teach Me

Campaign architecture: How to properly structure campaigns, ad sets, and ads for lead generation

Audience targeting: Custom audiences, lookalikes, interest stacking, broad vs. narrow strategies

Creative strategy: What makes scroll-stopping ad copy and creative, how to test hooks, angles, and formats

Budget & bidding: CBO vs. ABO, daily vs. lifetime budgets, cost caps, bid strategies

Advantage+ features: When to use Advantage+ campaigns, placements, and audience — and when NOT to

Pixel & CAPI: Conversion tracking setup, event optimization, and attribution

Reading data: Which metrics actually matter, when to kill an ad, when to scale, when to wait

Scaling strategies: How to go from testing to scaling without destroying performance



Requirements

- Minimum 2 years hands-on experience running
/Meta Ads (not just boosting posts)

- Experience with lead generation campaigns specifically (not just e-commerce/ROAS)

- Strong spoken English — you must be able to clearly explain concepts, not just execute them

- Patient teaching style — I’ll ask a lot of “why” questions and I expect thoughtful answers

- Available for screen share calls during Australian business hours (AEST) or flexible evening times

- Reliable internet connection for video calls with screen sharing



Nice to Have (Not Required)

- Experience with service business lead gen (clinics, medspas, dental, home services, etc.)

- Familiarity with Meta Instant Forms (Lead Ads)

- Experience coaching or training others (not just running ads yourself)

- Knowledge of Google Ads (I plan to learn this next, so a coach who knows both platforms is a bonus)



Compensation & Details

$15 AUD per hour (~$10.50 USD) paid per session

3 hours per week = $45 AUD/week (~$31.50 USD/week)

Paid weekly via Wise, PayPal, or direct transfer

Duration: 6-8 weeks initially, with potential to extend if needed

This is a part-time coaching engagement, not a full-time role



How to Apply

To apply, please do the following:

Record a 3-5 minute Loom video (loom.com — it’s free) walking me through how you would structure a lead generation campaign for a service business spending $500-$1,500/month on ads. Show your screen with Ads Manager open if possible. Explain your reasoning as you go.

In your application message, tell me: (a) How many years you’ve been running
 Ads, (b) The types of businesses/industries you’ve worked with, (c) One specific campaign result you’re proud of (budget, CPL, industry), and (d) Why you’d be a good teacher (not just a good media buyer).

Start your application with the word “MENTOR” so I know you read the full listing.



Applications without a Loom video will not be considered. I’m not looking for someone who just clicks buttons — I want someone who can explain the strategy behind every decision.

title: Agency Operations Manager
description:
We are looking for a highly capable operations leader who can take ownership inside a fast-growing marketing agency.



This is not a task-based VA role.



You will be responsible for helping run the internal systems that keep client campaigns, team members, and deliverables moving forward.



The ideal person is someone who thinks independently, solves problems, and takes pride in building organized systems that actually work.



If you need constant instruction or supervision, this role will not be a fit.



What You'll Be Responsible For



You will act as the operations hub for the agency.



Typical responsibilities include:



• Building landing pages for campaigns

• Designing marketing materials (flyers, graphics, event assets)

• Setting up campaign systems such as QR codes, booking calendars, and lead capture pages

• Onboarding new clients into our fulfillment systems

• Coordinating internal team members to ensure work is completed on time

• Leading the internal “pod” responsible for delivering results for each client

• Monitoring timelines and making sure nothing falls behind

• Observing the business and identifying operational bottlenecks

• Recommending and implementing solutions to improve efficiency



This role requires someone who can see problems before they happen and take initiative to solve them.



Example Projects You Might Run



One week you may be responsible for:



• Designing and launching a home show marketing campaign

• Building a landing page with a booking calendar and QR code

• Coordinating internal team members to prepare marketing assets

• Onboarding a new client into the agency system

• Identifying bottlenecks slowing down fulfillment and implementing solutions



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building systems and making organizations run smoothly.



Ideal Candidate



You are someone who:



• Thinks independently and solves problems without waiting for instructions

• Takes ownership and pride in the quality of your work

• Thinks in systems and processes

• Communicates clearly and professionally

• Can manage multiple moving parts at once

• Is comfortable leading projects and coordinating people



You are not just completing tasks.

You are helping run the operation.



Required Skills



• Experience with marketing or digital agencies

• Strong organizational and project management ability

• Excellent written communication



Bonus if you have experience with:



• CRM systems

• marketing campaign setup

• automation tools

• agency fulfillment workflows

• Landing page builders (GoHighLevel, Webflow, Wordpress, etc.)

• Basic design tools (Canva, Figma, Photoshop, etc.)



Compensation



Base Pay: 8-10 USD/hr



Performance Bonuses:



Monthly KPI bonuses including:



• $100 if all deadlines were met

• $50 if client satisfaction scores are high

• Additional bonuses based on operational improvements and performance



High performers will have opportunities for larger bonuses and leadership growth as the agency scales. You can earn an additional $500 USD per month in 



Who Should Apply



This role is perfect for someone who:



• Wants real responsibility

• Enjoys solving operational problems

• Wants to help build systems inside a growing company



If you're looking for a simple task-based VA job, this is not the right role.



How to Apply

title: Outbound Caller / Appointment Setter
description:
Job Description:



Type: Full-Time (40hrs/week) | 8 am to 5 pm Eastern

Location: Remote

Position Overview

title: Operations Virtual Assistant (Content Repurposing + Outreach + Brand Systems)| 20 HOURS/WEEK
description:
I’m Kimberly Strickland, an author and speaker building a growing brand around my upcoming book. I’m hiring a Virtual Assistant who thinks in operations: someone who can take high-level ideas and turn them into repeatable workflows, execute consistently, track progress, and help improve the system over time.



This is a long-term role for someone who communicates clearly, follows systems, takes ownership, and is willing to grow with the brand as responsibilities increase.



If you do not include the Required Proof Packet, I will not review your application.



Month 1 outcomes you will own

  - Domain + funnel setup

   -Connect my domain to 
 (DNS updates, redirects, troubleshooting)

   -Ensure links work properly and consistently

   -Brand consistency across platforms

   -Update social profiles with consistent branding and messaging

   -Align tone by platform:

   -YouTube = new audience growth through consistent publishing and clean edits

   -Pinterest = discovery traffic and book promotion graphics

   -LinkedIn = local Tampa presence and relationship nurturing

   -Instagram,
, TikTok = repurposed content for consistency, not the main growth engine



 marketing foundation

   Set up ConvertKit

   Build a basic newsletter workflow to nurture signups and book interest

   Weekly core priorities (recurring every week)



Content repurposing and consistency

   - YouTube: assist with publishing and basic long-form editing (clean cuts, quick transitions, consistent formatting, clear audio)

   - Shorts: create Shorts from long-form using tools I already have (OpusClip + Captions)

   - Repurpose: schedule content across platforms (IG, FB, TikTok, LinkedIn) using a consistent system

   - Pinterest: create and schedule simple book-related graphics and pins



Editing style

Clean, compelling, simple. No overproduction. No distracting effects. The focus is my communication.



Outreach and relationship operations (ongoing)

   - Build a podcast outreach pipeline: research, list-building, outreach, follow-up, booking coordination

   - Track local connections and follow-up tasks from people I meet in person

   - Outreach to book signing opportunities, bookstores, venues, author networks when needed

   - Maintain trackers so nothing falls through



Operations and workflow expectations

This role is Level 2 operations:

   - You help turn ideas into workflows

   - You execute the workflows

   - You help adjust and improve them based on what we learn



I will share resources and direction. We will collaborate to extract what matters and will be the most effective, then I need you to implement.



Example: If I send you free resources from YouTube Formula, you create a 30-day implementation plan, then we simplify it into weekly actions.



Tools:

   - Canva

   - YouTube Studio

   - OpusClip + Captions

   - Google Drive + Google Sheets

   - Trello (preferred for my personal task visibility I need for my action items)



Note: I prefer daily text check-ins and weekly live checkins.



Communication

   Daily check-in via message

   Weekly real-time meeting (30 minutes)

   Async work is fine as long as deadlines are met



Pay

   20 hours/week

   $400 to $500/month to start (increases as the business grows)

*Please include your expected monthly rate for 20 hours/week.*



*PROOF PACKET - QUESTIONS* To apply:

1. Put WILDERNESS at the top of your application and answer:

2. Your timezone and availability for 20 hours/week

3. Your operations experience: describe how you turn ideas into workflows and execute them

4. Your experience with outreach pipelines (podcasts, partnerships, bookings, follow-ups) and how you track them

5. Your editing experience (tools you use, what clean long-form editing means to you)

6. Confirm you can handle weekly recurring work and shifting priorities without dropping the ball

7. Confirm you are interested in growing long-term with increasing responsibility



Required Proof Packet Portfolio:

To be considered, include the following. If you skip these, I will not review the application. Be sure to provide context and explanation. 



A) Operations tracker sample (required)

   -Share a screenshot or sample of a Google Sheet tracker you’ve used (or create a blank sample) for outreach or lead tracking

   -Must include: Contact/Lead, Platform, Status, Last Contact Date, Next Follow-Up Date, Notes, Outcome



B) Content proof (original work only)

C) Branding proof

1 Canva sample (carousel, static post, thumbnail, or Pinterest pin)



D) Tech comfort (yes/no)

    Have you done DNS updates or domain connections before

 before

About Us



We are a fast-growing AI-powered SaaS platform in the influencer marketing space. Our product helps brands score creators on fake followers, brand safety, and engagement quality before they commit budget. We launched recently and are growing fast. Our customers are brands and agencies that spend on influencer and creator partnerships.



We're a small, fast-moving team and this is a hands-on role where your work directly impacts growth. If you thrive in early-stage environments where you wear multiple hats and see the results of your efforts quickly, this is for you.



The Role



We're looking for a full-time Digital Marketing & Social Media VA to help us find, qualify, and reach potential customers, and keep our social media presence active and growing.



This is a split role: roughly half your time on lead research and prospecting, half on social media management and content. You'll work closely with the founder and have real ownership over these channels from day one.



What You'll Do



Lead Research & Prospecting (50%)



- Research brands actively working with influencers and creators across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube

- Identify sponsored posts, gifted content, brand partnerships, and creator collaborations, logging the brands and creators you find in our internal system

- Qualify discovered brands against our target customer profile (DTC e-commerce, SaaS, subscription, health & wellness, beauty, fashion, food & beverage, and more)

- Find the right decision-maker at each qualified brand on LinkedIn (marketing managers, heads of marketing, founders, CMOs) and log their details in our system

- Send LinkedIn connection requests on behalf of tea
mbers using provided templates

- Engage with prospects' posts on LinkedIn (likes, thoughtful comments) to warm them up before outreach

- Help prepare and eventually send personalised outreach messages once connections are accepted

- Track and update prospect statuses as they move through the pipeline



Social Media & Content (50%)



- Manage and post to our 
pany page, repurposing blog content into native LinkedIn posts (3-4x per week)

- Manage our X/Twitter account, scheduling tweets, monitoring for replies and mentions, engaging with relevant conversations

- Monitor Reddit communities (r/influencermarketing, r/ecommerce, r/shopify and others) for relevant threads and flag them for the founder's personal response

- Compile a simple weekly report covering website traffic, social engagement,
 metrics, and pipeline progress

- Help maintain consistency across all social channels with brand voice and messaging



Who You Are



Must-haves:

- 3+ years of experience in digital marketing, social media management, or a related role

- Strong written and spoken English

- Experience with LinkedIn (posting, engagement, connection requests, prospecting)

- Comfortable with TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube - you can spot sponsored content and brand partnerships quickly

- Organised and detail-oriented - you'll be logging data across multiple systems daily

- Self-starter who can work independently without constant direction

- Comfortable working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment where processes are still being built

- Available to overlap at least 4 hours with UK GMT/BST timezone



Nice-to-haves:

- Experience in influencer marketing, either agency-side, brand-side, or as a creator yourself

- Experience with outreach campaigns (LinkedIn,
, or both)

- Familiarity with DTC / e-commerce brands and the creator economy

- Experience with tools like Instantly, Apollo, or similar outreach platforms

- Basic understanding of SaaS products and subscription business models



Working Arrangements



- Hours: Full-time (40 hours/week), at least 4 hours daily overlap with UK GMT/BST required

- Compensation: $
/month USD, depending on experience

- Location: Remote, Philippines-based

- Start: As soon as possible

- Trial: 1-week paid trial to ensure mutual fit before committing



How to Apply



Send us a short message covering:



1. A brief intro - who you are and your relevant experience

2. Have you worked with influencer marketing, DTC brands, or creator partnerships before? If so, how?

3. Describe a time you had to research and qualify leads or prospects. What was your process?

4. What social media platforms are you most experienced with, and what kind of content have you managed?

5. Your expected monthly salary in USD



Please do not send a generic cover letter. We read every application and value specific, thoughtful responses over length.

title: VIDEO EDITORS FOR LONGFORM, LONGTERM POSITION (OPEN)

We are seeking a highly experienced Lead Marketing Strategist to oversee and execute comprehensive marketing campaigns, with a strong emphasis on paid advertising and e-commerce growth.



You will work directly with the CEO, acting as a key partner in managing marketing operations and driving revenue through strategic planning, creative execution, and data-driven optimization.



This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-moving digital marketing environment, enjoys building funnels and offers, and wants to see their work directly impact business growth.

Horror



Romance



Fantasy



Coloring Books

Build sales funnels,
 campaigns, and paid advertising campaigns

Monitor key metrics such as:



CTR



Conversion rate



CPC



ROAS



Revenue performance



Analyze campaign data and optimize campaigns in real time



Adjust bids, keywords, placements, and targeting to improve results

Allocate spending strategically to maximize ROI and growth



Collaboration & Communication

E-commerce Expertise

Communicate with platform support teams when necessary



Required Skills & Experience



2+ years experience as a Marketing Lead or Marketing Specialist



Proven experience managing e-commerce marketing campaigns with paid advertising



Strong expertise with
 / Meta Ads



Experience with multiple advertising platforms including:



Google Ads



TikTok Ads



 / X Ads



YouTube Ads



Pinterest Ads



Experience with Shopify and WooCommerce platforms



Strong understanding of
 marketing strategies



Ability to create high-converting funnels and landing pages



Proficiency with marketing analytics tools and e-commerce software



Excellent data analysis and decision-making skills



Strong written and verbal English communication skills



Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects



Preferred Qualifications



Marketing platform certifications (Google Ads, Meta Blueprint, etc.)



Experience with marketing automation tools and CRM systems



Benefits



Compensation



$5 – $8 per hour depending on experience



Quarterly and annual performance bonuses



Work Structure



Full-time (40 hours per week)

Schedule

mbers LONG TERM. It is a time-consuming process teaching new tea
mbers the in's and outs of our company processes so we consider it an extreme inefficiency when we need to get someone new up to speed. We value loyalty and tenure-ship and most importantly, availability. Every tea
mber we hire, we would like to be with us until they retire. We are firm believers that nobody deserves to stay stagnant at any company. Every 1 year you are with us, you will be reviewed for a raise in compensation and the metrics we base the raise on are:

title: Coordination Specialist
description:
We are a growing real estate investment company looking for a motivated Virtual Assistant (VA) to help with lead generation and acquisitions for off-market properties. This role is ideal for someone who is confident speaking with homeowners, organized, and driven by performance-based commissions.



You will be responsible for contacting property owners, identifying motivated sellers, gathering property details, and nurturing leads until they are ready for a contract. Once the seller is ready to move forward, the deal will be handed off to our admin team for contract processing.



This position offers weekly pay plus a 5% commission on every deal that closes from your leads, giving you strong earning potential.





Responsibilities





Reach out to property owners through calls, SMS,
, or other outreach methods

Gather important property information including:



Property condition



Photos of the property



Ownership details



Property history



Reason for selling



Enter and update information in the CRM



Follow up with leads regularly until they are ready to move forward



Coordinate with the internal team once a seller is ready for a purchase agreement

Requirements





Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)



Comfortable speaking with homeowners on the phone



Experience in real estate, cold calling, or lead generation preferred



Familiarity with CRM systems is a plus



Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Ability to work full-time during U.S. business hours



Self-motivated and results-driven

title: Remote SDR – Outbound Sales Rep & Closer
description:
Title: Virtual Assistant – Sales Development Rep 

Type: Remote, offshore, full time (US Pacific hours overlap required)

Reports to: Sales Manager, Y Media Services



Mission:

Generate new advertising clients for a local marketing agency (print, digital, TV, direct mail) by prospecting local businesses, booking qualified meetings, and closing them into monthly marketing packages.



Key Responsibilities

Research and build targeted lists of local businesses (restaurants, home services, health/wellness, retail, professional services, etc.).



Execute a structured outbound cadence (calls,
, light social) to business owners and decision makers.



Set qualified appointments for marketing consultations and, where appropriate, run those consultations yourself via phone/video.



Conduct brief discovery: understand the business, their local marketing, and customer growth goals.



Present and explain Y Media’s advertising solutions (print, magazine, digital, TV, direct mail) using provided decks, rate sheets, and scripts.



Close new clients into clear packages (typically recurring monthly campaigns) following defined pricing and terms.



Follow up with all warm leads and no shows to re book and move them to a decision.



Keep the CRM fully updated: activities, notes, opportunity stages, deals closed.



Hit and exceed weekly/monthly KPIs for dials, conversations, appointments, and closed deals.



Daily Activities

Make a targeted number of outbound calls to new and existing leads each day.



Send personalized follow up and nurture
s using templates and your own wording.



Run short discovery/closing calls for interested prospects and present offers.



Confirm upcoming appointments with the CEO or yourself (depending on the process).



Log all work in the CRM and prepare simple daily and weekly performance reports.



Requirements

1–2+ years' experience in outbound sales, SDR, appointment setting, or closing (phone/Zoom).



Strong spoken and written English with a confident, friendly phone presence.



Comfortable handling objections and asking for the sale in a professional, respectful way.



Experience using CRM and online tools (e.g., pipeline tracking,
, calendar).



Ability to follow scripts and processes while sounding natural and adapting to each prospect.



Reliable internet connection, headset, and quiet working environment.



Self?motivated, organized, and able to manage your time and pipeline with minimal supervision.



Personality & Work Style

Hungry and results driven; enjoys seeing numbers and commissions grow.



Resilient and persistent with outbound calling and follow up.



Coachable, open to call reviews and feedback, willing to practice and improve.



Honest, reliable, and interested in a long term role with growing responsibility.



Compensation

Base: Lower fixed base (e.g., in the ~$500–$600/month range, depending on experience).



Commission: Tiered commission on every new client you close, with higher commission rates as you close more deals in a month.



Upside: Realistic total monthly earnings can reach around ~$1,200+ for consistent performance, with no cap on commission.



(Exact numbers and tiers will be shared during the interview and in the contract.)



How to Apply

Send your CV or a short voice recording (30–60 seconds) introducing yourself and sharing your outbound sales experience. In the subject of your application, please include YM575



Include a brief note on your past results (e.g., appointments booked/month, deals closed/month) and your available working hours in Pacific Time.

title: Car Rental Sales & Customer Service
description:
We are a Michigan-based car rental and auto repair company seeking a **highly reliable and detail-oriented virtual assistant** to handle customer inquiries, rental coordination, and problem resolution. You’ll work directly with customers who want to rent vehicles or are experiencing issues with their current rental.



This is a **full-time role (50 hours/week)**. You must be available to work **5 days per week, including Saturdays and Sundays**, with the ability to work as late as **10 PM Eastern Time**.



### Responsibilities:



* Respond promptly to inbound customer requests (calls, texts, and messages) regarding car rentals

* Troubleshoot rental issues (e.g., car won’t start, flat tire) and coordinate roadside assistance or towing

* Coordinate maintenance or repair needs with our auto repair team

* Schedule vehicles and update customer records

* Follow up with renters and maintain accurate communication logs



### Qualifications:



* Excellent spoken and written English

* Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills

* Experience in customer service, preferably with cars, rentals, or logistics

* High-speed internet, noise-canceling headset, and a quiet workspace

* Tech-savvy and able to learn new tools quickly (e.g., CRM, text/call software, GPS tracking systems)



---



### **IMPORTANT – HOW TO APPLY:**



To show us you pay attention to detail and follow instructions, **please include the word “watermelon” in your subject line or response title**.



At the **very top** of your application, answer all of the following:



1. What time zone you’re located in

2. What your expected hourly rate is (in USD)

3. What days/hours you are available to work (please confirm weekend availability)

4. Send me a recording discussing your work history starting in 2018. Start with 2018 and discuss where you worked, what the company did, and what your role in the company was and then why you left, and then move forward in time.



**Applications without these details or the word “watermelon” will be ignored.**

title: Paid Ads Manager (Meta & Google)
description:
Luxury Beauty Brands | Performance-Driven | Strategy-First



About the Role

Aavya Hair Studio – Luxury hair extension salon (local lead generation)



Aavya Hair – Premium raw hair extension e-commerce brand



Hair Launch Academy – Coaching programs, webinars, and live trainings for beauty professionals



This is not a plug-and-play role.



We are looking for someone who thinks strategically, understands consumer psychology, funnels, testing frameworks, and performance optimization, and can make smart decisions without constant supervision.



If you need step-by-step instructions for every campaign, this role is not the right fit.



Key Responsibilities

Paid Advertising Strategy & Execution



Plan, launch, and scale Meta 
 & Instagram) and Google Ads campaigns



Develop custom strategies for each brand (local services, e-commerce, and webinar funnels)



Select the right campaign objectives, audiences, and creative angles



Build structured testing frameworks for creatives, copy, audiences, offers, and landing pages



Continuously optimize campaigns based on real performance data



Funnel & Conversion Optimization



Understand the entire marketing funnel:

Ad ? Landing Page ? Booking / Checkout / Webinar



Work alongside automation and CRM systems (GoHighLevel preferred)



Identify conversion drop-off points and recommend improvements



Collaborate on landing page testing and offer optimization



Creative Direction (Beyond Just Running Ads)



Provide clear creative direction for ad production (hooks, angles, formats, UGC styles)



Identify and scale winning creatives



Understand what performs in beauty, luxury, and education markets



Test a variety of formats: short-form video, static, carousel, storytelling ads



Data, Testing & Performance Optimization



Monitor performance metrics daily:



CPL



CPA



ROAS



CTR



Conversion Rate



Scale winners and cut underperforming campaigns quickly



Understand attribution models and platform data differences



Deliver clear performance reports with actionable insights



What We're Looking For (Non-Negotiable)



You must:



Have proven experience managing profitable Meta and Google Ads campaigns



Understand local service ads, e-commerce, and webinar funnels



Be comfortable testing, analyzing, and making decisions independently



Know how to interpret data and adjust strategy without waiting for instructions



Be proactive, organized, and detail-oriented



Communicate clearly and professionally



Be capable of managing multiple ad accounts and brands simultaneously



Strong Preference If You



Have experience with beauty, salons, e-commerce, or coaching programs



Have worked with GoHighLevel, Shopify, Stripe, or booking calendars



Understand luxury brand positioning and premium pricing



Have scaled campaigns beyond starter-level budgets



What Success Looks Like in This Role



Salon campaigns consistently generate qualified booked consultations



E-commerce campaigns drive profitable sales and repeat customers



Coaching campaigns fill webinars, live trainings, and high-ticket programs



Testing frameworks are consistent and data-driven



You bring strategic ideas, new angles, and growth opportunities



Reporting & Communication



Weekly performance reports covering:



What worked



What didn’t



What will be tested next



Clear recommendations for scaling or pivoting campaigns



Strong ownership mindset — you treat ad spend like it's your own money



Compensation



Competitive monthly retainer (based on experience)



Performance bonuses for strong results



Long-term opportunity managing multiple brands with growing ad budgets



Fully remote position



How to Apply



To be considered, please include:



A brief overview of your paid ads experience



Examples of campaigns you have managed (industry, objective, results)



Your approach to testing and scaling campaigns



Why you believe you’re a strong fit for luxury beauty and education brands



Applicants who submit generic applications will not be considered.



Final Note



We are building serious brands with serious revenue goals.



This role is for a high-level media buyer who wants ownership, autonomy, and the opportunity to scale impactful brands.



If you’re strategic, analytical, and performance-driven — we want to hear from you.



Please send your CV and application to:

title: CEO Assistant for Chain Spa in US
description:
**Location:** Remote www.cloud9.spa

**Employment Type:** Full-Time



Please do not apply for the following situation

- You are an agency

- You are working multiple jobs

- You are jumping from job to job and cannot hold a job for less than 2 years

- You do not have US employer references

- You have less than 3 years of full time EA experience



About Us:

We are a rapidly growing chain of family-friendly spas committed to exceptional client experience, employee development, and operational excellence. As we continue to expand, we are seeking a highly experienced and proactive HR Specialist & Executive Assistant to the CEO to support high-level strategic initiatives and drive team success across our multi-location business.



---



### Key Responsibilities



**HR & Team Development**



* Assist the CEO in developing and implementing a manager training program tailored for the spa industry.

* Conduct bi-monthly one-on-one meetings with front desk staff and managers to promote professional growth and address operational concerns.

* Review and enhance career development tracks for receptionists and managers.

* Lead quarterly performance reviews for all front desk staff and managers.

* Collaborate in project performance reviews, including tracking milestones and outcomes.



**Strategic Communications & Project Management**



* Communicate major project announcements, updates, and evaluations on behalf of the CEO.

* Support project execution and help ensure follow-through and accountability across departments.

* Manage and enhance online reputation by reviewing customer feedback, drafting responses, and proposing service resolutions.



**Executive Support**



* Manage the CEO’s calendar, travel, and strategic planning needs.

* Conduct business research to support decision-making and strategic initiatives.

* Assist in evaluating completed projects, including effectiveness, team performance, and client impact.

* Provide assistance on special projects and operational improvements across the organization.



---



### Qualifications



* 5–10 years of HR experience in an American business environment, preferably in multi-location operations.

* Proven experience in developing managers, leading teams, and implementing performance management systems.

* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; must be articulate, persuasive, and detail-oriented.

* Strong project management and organizational abilities.

* Demonstrated success in managing by objectives and driving results.

* High level of discretion, emotional intelligence, and ability to handle confidential information.

* Background in wellness, hospitality, or service-based industries is a plus.



---



What We Offer



* Opportunity to work closely with executive leadership

* Collaborative and mission-driven team environment

* Growth potential in a fast-scaling company



---



How to Apply:

Please submit your resume, a brief cover letter, a self intro video, and some project summaries demonstrating your ability to communicate effectively and manage complex initiatives.

* Support project execution and help ensure follow-through and accountability across departments.

* Manage and enhance online reputation by reviewing customer feedback, drafting responses, and proposing service resolutions.



**Executive Support**



* Manage the CEO’s calendar, travel, and strategic planning needs.

* Conduct business research to support decision-making and strategic initiatives.

* Assist in evaluating completed projects, including effectiveness, team performance, and client impact.

* Provide assistance on special projects and operational improvements across the organization.



---



### Qualifications



* 5–10 years of HR experience in an American business environment, preferably in multi-location operations.

* Proven experience in developing managers, leading teams, and implementing performance management systems.

* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; must be articulate, persuasive, and detail-oriented.

* Strong project management and organizational abilities.

* Demonstrated success in managing by objectives and driving results.

* High level of discretion, emotional intelligence, and ability to handle confidential information.

* Background in wellness, hospitality, or service-based industries is a plus.



---



What We Offer



* Opportunity to work closely with executive leadership

* Collaborative and mission-driven team environment

* Growth potential in a fast-scaling company



---



How to Apply:

Please submit your resume, a brief cover letter, a self intro video, and some project summaries demonstrating your ability to communicate effectively and manage complex initiatives.
